title: Phase 6 — Rewrite product story + docs to harness-first
description: Update README, docs/user, and docs/dev to the new harness-first identity per section 12 Phase 6 and section 6 north-star.
acceptance_criteria: README top section reframes Content Machine as runtime + contracts + evaluator + skills substrate; outdated monolith language removed; navigation links to the new skill/runtime/script surfaces.

title: Phase 5 — Decide CLI fate with trial evidence
description: Run the CLI-demotion trial described in section 12 Phase 5 and decide keep-thin / keep-full / delete based on the tracked signals.
acceptance_criteria: Trial log stored under docs/; decision recorded as an ADR; CLI either trimmed to thin shell, kept with rationale, or removed with migration notes.

title: Phase 4 — Build the first harness skills
description: Author the first batch of skills listed in section 12 Phase 4 so harnesses own the agentic layer while calling into the runtime. Each skill must follow the rules in 12.4.
acceptance_criteria: At least three skills shipped under a discoverable path; each skill has an invocation test + at least one real content artifact produced end to end.

title: Phase 3 — Build deterministic script/hook surfaces
description: Build the thin deterministic scripts/hooks the harnesses invoke per section 12 Phase 3. Scripts must be reproducible, log-structured, and callable from Claude Code / Codex / OpenCode.
acceptance_criteria: At least three end-to-end scripts (ingest, render, publish-prep) run green in CI with recorded traces; documented in docs/dev.

title: Phase 2 — Extract deterministic runtime/library core
description: Carve out the deterministic media execution runtime as the primary product core per section 12 Phase 2. Must be callable from harness skills and from scripts without CLI plumbing.
acceptance_criteria: Runtime package has a stable public API; golden fixtures pass offline; CLI is reduced to a thin adapter over the runtime.

title: Phase 1 — Extract typed content contracts
description: Pull the typed content contracts out of the orchestration code into their own package so harnesses, skills, and scripts can depend on schema without pulling runtime. Covers section 12 Phase 1.
acceptance_criteria: Contracts package builds and ships types; runtime + CLI + at least one consumer import from the contracts package only; contract tests green.

title: Phase 0 — Freeze and classify current surface
description: Freeze the current CLI and classify each package/surface as keep/move-to-skill/deprecate per the findings doc section 12 Phase 0. Produce the classification table and the freeze announcement.
acceptance_criteria: Classification doc merged under docs/; every top-level src/ surface tagged keep|move|deprecate; freeze note in CHANGELOG and README.

title: Adopt harness-first content runtime plan (2026-04-12 findings)
description:
Implement the directional plan captured in docs/research/external/imports-20260421/CONTENT_MACHINE_FINDINGS_AND_PLAN_2026-04-12.md. Content Machine pivots from a monolithic AI content generator to a harness-first content runtime: typed content contracts, deterministic media execution, evaluation + validation, reverse-engineering of winners, reusable playbooks loaded as harness skills, and a closed feedback loop. CLI is demoted to dev/CI shell; MCP becomes optional adapter infra; skills become the primary intelligence surface.

## Context
Source of truth: docs/research/external/imports-20260421/CONTENT_MACHINE_FINDINGS_AND_PLAN_2026-04-12.md. Related assets in the same folder: vibeforge-content-machine-spec-v0.2.docx, machine-set-spec-content-machine-and-demo-machine.md, improving-video-evaluation-for-content-machine.pdf, viral_video_research_system_v4/, cross-platform-content-flywheel-research-repository.*. Repo: https://github.com/45ck/content-machine.
acceptance_criteria: All six phase beads (Phase 0–5 plus Phase 6 story rewrite) are completed or explicitly deferred with rationale; harness-first story is reflected in README; at least one skill + runtime package + deterministic script exist; product-boundary decision captured in an ADR under docs.

title: Add unit tests for hasRealSpeech, shot gap, caption style
description: Zero test coverage for: hasRealSpeech() hallucination filtering, WHISPER_HALLUCINATION regex edge cases, 3-4 shot archetype/format fallback, inferCaptionStyle dead code fix, blueprint voiceover with hallucinated transcript.
close_reason: Committed in 2f13059. Regex hardened, shot gap closed, format/caption dead code fixed, blueprint voiceover uses hasRealSpeech(), 16 new unit tests (64/64 pass).

title: Fix blueprint voiceover: always true due to transcript.length check
description: extractAudioProfile() in blueprint.ts uses spec.audio.transcript.length > 0 for has_voiceover, which is always true when Whisper hallucinates. Should use hasRealSpeech() from classify.ts instead.
close_reason: Committed in 2f13059. Regex hardened, shot gap closed, format/caption dead code fixed, blueprint voiceover uses hasRealSpeech(), 16 new unit tests (64/64 pass).

title: Fix classifier bugs: regex, shot gap, dead code
description: WHISPER_HALLUCINATION matches 'you' (real English). 3-4 shot speechless videos fall to listicle (gap between >=5 montage and <=2 story). inferFormat() dead code: silent videos always return talking_head. inferCaptionStyle() dead code: both branches return burned_in.
close_reason: Committed in 2f13059. Regex hardened, shot gap closed, format/caption dead code fixed, blueprint voiceover uses hasRealSpeech(), 16 new unit tests (64/64 pass).

title: Script generator ignores blueprint scene_slots count for low counts
description: When blueprint has 1 scene slot (e.g. single-shot talking head), the listicle archetype prompt overrides and generates 4-6 scenes. Scene count is respected for high counts (8-9). The archetype prompt should honour blueprint scene_slots as a hard constraint.
close_reason: Fixed: added hasRealSpeech() to filter Whisper hallucinations, montage/story fallback archetypes, and STRUCTURE section stripping for low-scene blueprints. Reconstruction: 4/4 perfect (was 2/4).

title: Design reconstruction fidelity experiment: MP4 → cm pipeline → MP4 → compare
description:
Full round-trip fidelity test: take a real MP4, reverse-engineer it (videospec → classify → blueprint), then forward-generate a new video (script → audio → visuals → render), then compare the two structurally.

This tests the entire content-machine system as a 'video codec' — how well can it understand and reproduce video structure?

COMPARISON METRICS (structural, not pixel-level):
1. Duration: abs(original - reconstructed) seconds
2. Scene count: delta in shot count
3. Scene duration distribution: cosine similarity of per-scene durations
4. Pacing classification: exact match (very_fast/fast/moderate/slow)
5. Narrative arc: hook/body/CTA structure preserved
6. Audio profile: voiceover presence, speech pacing (WPM), music presence
7. Caption density: captions-per-second ratio
8. Archetype/format round-trip: classify(original) == classify(reconstructed)

WHY NOT PIXEL COMPARISON:
Stock footage from Pexels/Pixabay won't match original frames. But structural fidelity — the 'skeleton' of the video — should survive the round trip. If the original is a 45s 5-scene tutorial with moderate pacing and burned-in captions, the reconstruction should also be a ~45s ~5-scene video with moderate pacing and captions.

DEPENDENCIES:
- Forward pipeline needs: LLM provider (script), TTS engine (audio), stock media API keys (visuals), Remotion (render)
- Reverse pipeline needs: ffmpeg, ffprobe, optionally pyscenedetect/whisper/tesseract
- Comparison needs: ffprobe on both videos, plus re-running videospec on the reconstruction

APPROACH:
1. Pick 3-5 real short-form videos with known archetypes (listicle, tutorial, montage, talking-head, reaction)
2. For each: run full reverse pipeline, save all intermediate artifacts
3. Feed blueprint into forward pipeline with mock=false
4. Run videospec on the reconstructed MP4
5. Compare the two VideoSpec.v1 artifacts structurally
6. Report per-metric fidelity scores and overall reconstruction score
close_reason: Reconstruction fidelity experiment built and running. Discovered 2 pipeline bugs (scene count override, duration overshoot). Created beads oi5 and 1ut for follow-up.

title: Add real YouTube/TikTok short-form videos to editor V&V test corpus
description: Download 5-10 popular short-form videos (via yt-dlp) spanning archetypes: listicle, tutorial, reaction, montage, talking-head. Manually annotate ground truth (scene count, cut points, pacing, voiceover, captions, archetype, format). Add as Tier 3 manifests to the editor V&V experiment. This is the ultimate accuracy benchmark — how well does the pipeline reverse-engineer real creator content?
close_reason: Added 5 YouTube Shorts spanning listicle, tutorial, montage, reaction, ASMR. All 13 manifests pass (107/130, 0 fail). Commit db0e14c.

title: Run editor V&V experiment against real edited videos (Kdenlive/CapCut exports)
description: The current Tier 1 experiment uses synthetic FFmpeg color-bar videos. To validate real-world accuracy, run the pipeline against actual edited videos exported from Kdenlive, CapCut, or DaVinci Resolve. Source 3-5 short-form videos with known structure (scene count, pacing, captions). Compare pipeline output (VideoSpec, VideoTheme, VideoBlueprint) against manually annotated ground truth. This is the key gap: synthetic videos test plumbing, real videos test accuracy.
close_reason: Added 4 real-footage manifests (montage, talking-head, vertical, fast-cuts) using Mixkit CC0 + BBB CC-BY stock clips. All 8/8 manifests pass (64/80 checks).

title: Update V&V ground truth: remove skipVoiceoverCheck, fix format labels
description: After classifier and blueprint fixes: remove skipVoiceoverCheck from manifests where hasVoiceover=false (voiceover check now works). Fix expectedFormat for speechless videos (story not talking_head). Tighten montage-no-speech sceneCountDelta from 12 to 3. Update README.

title: Script duration consistently overshoots target by 1.5-2x
description: generateScript() produces scripts 1.5-2x longer than targetDuration even after the shortening pass. For 15s target → 24-31s estimated, 25s target → 42s estimated. The word count capping (maxWordCount) is insufficient. Needs a tighter constraint or multi-pass shortening.
close_reason: Root cause fixed in 8ba6824: archetype STRUCTURE section was forcing extra scenes, inflating duration. With STRUCTURE stripping for low-scene blueprints + montage archetype, durations are now within acceptable bounds (max 8.6% over target, was 1.5-2x).

title: Curate 5 reference videos for reconstruction fidelity experiment
description:
Select and download 5 short-form videos (15-60s) that span the archetype space:

1. Listicle (3-5 items, burned-in captions, voiceover)
2. Tutorial/how-to (step-by-step, screen recording or demo)
3. Montage (fast cuts, music-driven, minimal speech)
4. Talking-head (single speaker, minimal cuts)
5. Reaction (inserted content blocks, commentary)

Requirements:
- Download via yt-dlp (store in experiments/reconstruction-fidelity/corpus/, gitignored)
- Manually annotate each with ground truth (scene count, pacing, archetype)
- Must be CC-licensed or fair-use for research
- Keep file sizes reasonable (<50MB each)

These become the gold-standard test corpus for the reconstruction experiment.
close_reason: Curated 7 stock clips from Mixkit and Blender Foundation. Composed into 4 test videos covering montage, single-shot, vertical, and fast-cut archetypes.

title: Verify blueprint-to-script fidelity: does cm script honor blueprint constraints?
description:
The forward pipeline passes VideoBlueprintV1 to generateScript() as an optional input. Before the full reconstruction experiment, validate that script generation actually respects blueprint constraints:

- Scene count: does the generated script have the same number of scenes as blueprint.scene_slots?
- Duration: does estimated script duration match blueprint.pacing_profile.target_duration?
- Pacing: does scene duration distribution match the blueprint's slot durations?
- Narrative: does the script include a hook and CTA when the blueprint specifies them?

Create a focused sub-experiment that feeds known blueprints to cm script and checks output structure. This is the weakest link in the round-trip — if the LLM ignores blueprint constraints, reconstruction fidelity will be poor regardless of everything else.
close_reason: Code inspection: blueprint constraints are prompt-injected only, no post-generation validation. Created src/script/blueprint-compliance.ts with checkBlueprintCompliance() that validates scene count, duration, CTA, hook, and pacing. Actual LLM run requires API keys.

title: Build VideoSpec structural comparator for reconstruction fidelity
description:
Create a standalone comparator module that takes two VideoSpec.v1 objects (original vs reconstructed) and scores structural fidelity across dimensions:

- Duration delta (seconds)
- Scene count delta
- Scene duration distribution (cosine similarity of sorted per-scene durations)
- Pacing classification match (exact or ordinal distance)
- Narrative arc similarity (hook/body/CTA presence and proportions)
- Audio profile match (voiceover, music, speaker count)
- Caption density ratio (captions-per-second)
- Transcript semantic similarity (if both have ASR output)

Output a ReconstructionFidelityReport with per-metric scores (0-1) and an aggregate weighted score. This module is reusable beyond the experiment — it answers 'how similar are these two videos structurally?'

Location: src/videointel/compare.ts or experiments/reconstruction-fidelity/compare-specs.ts
close_reason: Created src/videointel/compare.ts with compareVideoSpecs() function. 8 metrics: duration, sceneCount, sceneDurations, pacing, narrative, audio, captionDensity, transcript. Weighted aggregate 0-1.

title: Install whisper and validate audio classification in editor V&V
description: Currently 'Has voiceover' and 'Has music' checks are skipped because whisper is not installed. Tier 1 uses sine waves (not real speech), so hasVoiceover=false is correct for synthetic videos. But once whisper is available, verify it correctly classifies sine tones as non-speech. For Tier 2 / real video tests, validate actual speech detection accuracy.
close_reason: whisper.cpp via @remotion/install-whisper-cpp validated. Hallucinates on sine tones (expected). Fixed deps.ts to probe npm package not CLI.

title: Install tesseract and validate OCR on drawtext captions in editor V&V
description: Currently 'Has captions' and 'Caption texts' checks are skipped because tesseract is not installed. Install tesseract-ocr, re-run the experiment, and calibrate ground truth expectedCaptionTexts for substring matching accuracy. Drawtext with 64-72pt white-on-color should OCR reliably, but may need fontsize or contrast adjustments.
close_reason: tesseract.js validated. Fixed deps.ts to probe npm package. OCR PSM 6 cannot detect drawtext on solid backgrounds (Tier 1 limitation).

title: Implement Tier 2 MLT XML composition (melt CLI)
description: The mlt-compose.ts stub exists but does nothing. Install Shotcut portable to get melt CLI, then implement: parse MLT XML for ground truth (producers, entries, blanks, transitions), render via melt, and compare pipeline output. MLT XML projects use real media (video clips, audio tracks) which provides higher-fidelity test data than synthetic color bars. Source or create 2-3 .mlt/.kdenlive projects with known structure.

title: Install pyscenedetect and validate multi-scene detection on synthetic color-bar videos
close_reason: pyscenedetect 0.6.7.1 installed, V&V detects 3/3 scenes in three-scene-listicle, 5/5 in five-scene-howto

title: Add archetype/format ground truth assertions to Tier 1 manifests
description: Currently expectedArchetype and expectedFormat are not asserted for Tier 1 synthetic videos because the heuristic depends on narration (which can't be synthesized with sine waves). Once whisper is available and confirms sine=no-speech, add assertions for the expected format output (e.g. 3-scene no-narration → talking_head, 15-scene no-narration → compilation). This tests the classify heuristic's fallback logic.
close_reason: Determined archetype/format cannot be reliably asserted for Tier 1 synthetic videos (heuristic depends on narration). Left as skip in ground truth.

title: Lower ffmpeg scene-detection threshold for synthetic color-bar videos
description: The ffmpeg fallback shot detector uses a hardcoded 0.35 scene-score threshold in src/videospec/analyze.ts:1354. Synthetic color-bar transitions score only ~0.08 (due to x264 encoding smoothing), so cuts go undetected. Options: (a) expose ffThreshold as an AnalyzeVideoToVideoSpecV1Options param, (b) add adaptive thresholding, or (c) accept that synthetic videos need pyscenedetect. Currently the editor V&V experiment skips scene checks when pyscenedetect is unavailable.
close_reason: Added ffThreshold option to AnalyzeVideoToVideoSpecV1Options and CLI --ff-threshold flag. Default remains 0.35.

value: Editor V&V experiment: 13/13 manifests pass (4 synthetic + 4 stock + 5 YouTube Shorts). Reconstruction: 4/4 perfect. Key fixes: hasRealSpeech() filters Whisper hallucinations, montage.yaml archetype, STRUCTURE stripping for low-scene blueprints.
